payload.content: prd_review for wiring the real configured reviewer panel into mergeability calibration so S_full is measured separately from S_probe. All four promise contracts (P1 configured-panel wiring, P2 oracle isolation, P3 fail-closed availability, P4 replayable evidence) ground to real source seams. Genuine net-new work: configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel have zero occurrences in mergeability_bench.py, so the production roster is not yet wired. Out-of-scope and report-only invariants explicit and verified hardcoded False. Accept.

assumptions_to_verify: TDD will start RED at run_paired_acceptance_pilot with configured-panel mode and fake reviewers (per Testing Decisions), not at a helper-only adapter
Implementation will route the oracle-leak block via _public_input_oracle_refs before reviewer invocation (P2/Finding 3)
Default calibration produces an explicit unavailable S_full (never accepted) when reviewers cannot run (P3)
contradictions_checked: Checked whether this repeats the prior committed full-gate-estimand work: NO -- that wired only an injected panel; configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel have zero occurrences in mergeability_bench.py, so production-roster wiring is genuinely new
Checked whether report-only/non-applyable claims contradict source: NO -- invariants are hardcoded False at :1038-1051 and :1283-1288
Checked whether claimed seams exist: all 6 confirmed via Grep/Read
missing_evidence: No execution receipt or passing test output (expected; code not yet written at prd_review)
Exact default-availability behavior when configured reviewers are unreachable is deferred to TDD/impl
critical_review.strongest_objection: The PRD is forward-looking and contains no validation receipt, and there is a mild coherence tension: production construction is told to call configured_reviewers (live reviewer infrastructure), while Testing Decisions forbid live Cursor/Codex/oracle execution by default. How the default calibration behaves when no live infra is present is not fully specified at PRD level.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that configured_reviewers/evaluate_reviewer_panel are already wired into the calibration bench (making this a repeated handoff/FM-1.3), or that any of the four promise contracts cannot bind to a real seam, or that the report-only invariants are not actually enforced False in source.

Accept for implementation.

criteria: 5 named TDD tests run and pass: met
No regression in existing reviewer-panel/full-gate/paired-report tests: met
Implementation diff covers PRD P1-P4 (panel wiring, leak isolation, fail-closed availability, replayable evidence): met
Public boundary unchanged for default callers: met
confidence.rationale: Both the 5 TDD tests and the 8 regression tests pass against the implementation. Public-boundary tests exercise observable report rows and arm summaries, not internal helpers. Oracle-leak path verified by monkeypatched fake adapter that records calls. Slight residual: configured-panel mode delegates to existing registry helpers, so any defect in those helpers' aggregation could propagate undetected.

payload.content: Wired configured reviewer panel into run_paired_acceptance_pilot via ConfiguredReviewerPanelOptions and build_configured_reviewer_panel. S_full measured separately via real reviewer registry adapters with oracle-leak short-circuit, unavailable-not-accept normalization, replayable per-row evidence (reviewer_results, packet_refs, packet_sha256, panel_decision, s_probe_vs_s_full_disagreement), and report-only invariants preserved. 5 TDD tests pass; 8 existing reviewer-panel tests still pass.

assumptions_to_verify: evaluate_reviewer_panel conservative aggregation behaves correctly under all-accept, mixed, and all-unavailable inputs (covered by fake-adapter tests, but real-adapter behavior assumed identical).
_public_input_oracle_refs covers all leak vectors that could appear in a reviewer packet (existing leak detector reused, not modified).
contradictions_checked: Verified backwards compat: default reviewer_panel_mode='custom' still accepts the existing reviewer_panel callable and existing tests pass.
Verified report-only invariants: configured_reviewer_panel_active does not flip metric_applyable to True (test_configured_panel_calibration_remains_report_only asserts policy evolution returns []).
missing_evidence: End-to-end run against a real ReviewerAdapter (e.g., invoke_cursor_agent) rather than fake adapters — out of scope for unit tests and explicitly noted as a risk in the implementation plan.
Performance characterization of configured-panel pilot at non-trivial cohort sizes.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The configured-panel adapter is primarily an orchestration layer over reviewer_registry helpers (configured_reviewers, independent_reviewer_results_from_review_results, evaluate_reviewer_panel). Net-new behavior is the mode wiring, oracle-leak short-circuit at the adapter entry, and surfaced evidence fields — not novel aggregation logic. A subtle aggregation bug in evaluate_reviewer_panel would not be caught here.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing pytest run on any of the 5 TDD nodeids or the 8 regression nodeids, or evidence that the oracle-leak detector misses a key the PRD listed as forbidden.
